title: triticum timopheevii subsp. armeniacum
intro: Triticum timopheevii subsp. armeniacum, also known as Armenian wheat or wild wheat, is an ancient subspecies of wheat that is native to the mountainous regions of the Caucasus. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, making it useful for treating digestive issues and skin conditions. The health benefits of Triticum timopheevii subsp. armeniacum include reducing inflammation and improving immune function, as well as providing antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to promote overall health and well-being.
